novel_features.name: Weekly Drift Report
novel_features.description: Diff this week's domain + keyword snapshot against last week's to see what moved, without re-spending credits on the data you already pulled.
novel_features.rationale: Requires two persisted snapshots in the local SQLite store; Semrush keeps history server-side but does not expose row-level diffs as an API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is asked 'what changed for this domain this week', this is the single command that answers it from the local store with no API spend.

novel_features.name: Snapshot Tag + Diff
novel_features.description: Tag the current local-store state of any resource (e.g. monday-baseline) and later diff any two tags to see exactly what moved between them.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local SQLite — adds a snapshot_labels table; no upstream endpoint required.
novel_features.example: semrush-pp-cli snapshot tag monday && semrush-pp-cli snapshot diff monday today --select phrase,position --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent reason about state across multiple weeks of conversations without re-paying for the same data.

novel_features.name: Backlink Delta Since Last Sync
novel_features.description: Show only the referring domains and backlinks first seen in a window, so the 'did anything new link to us this week' question is a one-liner.
novel_features.rationale: Uses the first_seen column persisted by the backlinks sync; no Semrush endpoint computes this delta.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Saves an agent from re-pulling and diffing two full backlink reports to answer a recency question.

novel_features.name: Budget Ledger
novel_features.description: Every API call's documented unit cost is logged locally; budget rolls up spend by day, command, resource, and projects month-end burn from the current balance probe.
novel_features.rationale: Synthesizes per-call cost into a queryable credit_log table the Semrush API does not expose; balance probe is free.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent before a request 'this would cost N units' and after a month 'here is where the units went' — neither answerable from the upstream alone.

novel_features.name: Keyword Gap
novel_features.description: Set-difference of organic keywords across two or more domains in the same database, with intersection and unique-to-each modes.
novel_features.rationale: The Semrush API has no set-difference endpoint; this joins two domain-keyword responses locally.
novel_features.example: semrush-pp-cli keyword gap myco.com competitor1.com competitor2.com --database us --kd-max 40 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the manual two-CSV VLOOKUP that every SEO consultant does by hand.
novel_features.group: Cross-domain joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: Backlink Gap
novel_features.description: Find referring domains that link to a competitor but not to you, filtered by authority score.
novel_features.rationale: Left-anti-join across two persisted referring_domains rowsets — single API calls cannot return this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Directly surfaces link-building targets an agent can hand to an outreach workflow.
novel_features.group: Cross-domain joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: Audit Triage
novel_features.description: Rank Site Audit pages by weighted issue severity (errors x3, warnings x1, notices x0.1) so you fix the highest-impact pages first.
novel_features.rationale: Joins audit_issues x audit_pages x issue catalog locally; the upstream returns raw counts but no weighted page ranking.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns a Site Audit dump into a prioritized action list an agent can hand to the next step.
novel_features.group: SEO content patterns no competitor exposes

novel_features.name: Position Drift
novel_features.description: Window-function over Position Tracking snapshots: which keywords moved more than N positions, dropped off page 1, or entered page 1, per region and device.
novel_features.rationale: Requires multiple tracking_positions snapshots joined locally; the upstream gives latest-only or static emails.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'what tracked keywords moved this month' in one command — the question Position Tracking emails fail to answer.

novel_features.name: Country Fan-out
novel_features.description: Run Domain Overview across multiple country databases in one shot, persist all rows with the database key so later cross-region drift queries work.
novel_features.rationale: Sequential fan-out across N database values with composite-key persistence; the upstream endpoint is single-database per call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent answer 'how does this domain perform across our top 5 markets' as one call rather than five.
novel_features.group: Cross-domain joins the API can't do

novel_features.name: SERP Feature Monitor
novel_features.description: Time-series view of which SERP features (featured snippet, People Also Ask, video, image pack) have appeared and disappeared for a tracked keyword.
novel_features.rationale: Surfaces the SERP-feature flag columns already persisted by keyword organic-serp as a time series; the upstream returns latest-only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells a content-strategy agent when an opportunity (e.g. featured snippet slot opening) appeared or closed.
novel_features.group: SEO content patterns no competitor exposes

novel_features.name: Cannibalization Detector
novel_features.description: List phrases where the same domain ranks for multiple URLs (a known SEO problem) with the URLs and their respective positions.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local GROUP BY phrase HAVING count(distinct url) > 1 over keyword_positions; not an API endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces a high-value SEO problem in one command — most consultants charge to find this.
novel_features.group: SEO content patterns no competitor exposes

novel_features.name: Audit Regression Watch
novel_features.description: Diff the latest two Site Audit snapshots for a project: new issue IDs introduced, issues resolved, count delta per severity.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local diff over two audit_snapshots rows; upstream emails are static and don't expose issue-ID-level deltas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent tell you exactly what your tech-SEO state regressed on between two crawls.

narrative.headline: Every Semrush Analytics + Projects feature, plus a local SQLite store and cross-domain joins no other Semrush tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Wraps the Semrush v3 Analytics + Projects APIs with a real local store so weekly drift, keyword gap, backlink gap, and Site Audit triage become one-shot queries instead of multi-tab CSV diffs. Every API unit you spend is logged to a queryable budget ledger, and offline FTS5 search over everything you've ever synced means follow-up questions cost zero credits.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set `SEMRUSH_API_KEY` from your Subscription Info → API Units page. The CLI uses the same v3 query-param auth as the official docs and the free balance endpoint as its doctor check, so you never spend credits to verify the key works.

quickstart.comment: free — verifies the API key and prints remaining unit balance
one-row Domain Overview, 10 units, persisted to the local store
top 50 organic keywords, persisted for later drift queries
tag current store state so you can diff against it next week
after a second sync: structured diff of what moved
where did your credits go this month

troubleshoots.symptom: ERROR 132 :: API UNITS BALANCE IS ZERO
troubleshoots.fix: Out of credits. Run `semrush-pp-cli account balance` to confirm, then top up at https://www.semrush.com/billing-admin/profile/subscription/api-units

troubleshoots.symptom: ERROR 131 :: LIMIT EXCEEDED on a single report
troubleshoots.fix: Per-report quota hit. Re-run with `--limit` lower than the default, or wait for the report-level limit to reset.
Rate-limited at 10 RPS. The CLI's built-in limiter handles this; if you see it persistently, drop concurrency below 10.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor reports auth_invalid (HTTP 200 but empty body)
troubleshoots.fix: Key is malformed or revoked. Re-copy from Subscription Info → API Units.

troubleshoots.symptom: Local drift query returns 'no prior snapshot'
troubleshoots.fix: Run `semrush-pp-cli sync --resources domain,keyword` at least twice with a gap between, or use `snapshot tag` to record an explicit baseline.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need to query Semrush data the same way more than once — weekly drift, keyword gap, backlink gap, Site Audit regression — or when you're orchestrating Semrush through an agent that should not re-spend credits on data already pulled. It's not the right tool for one-off ad-hoc lookups (the official Semrush UI is faster) or for Trends API workflows (out of scope by design).

recipes.title: Monday baseline + Friday diff
recipes.explanation: After syncing your tracked domains and keywords, tag the snapshot. Re-sync on Friday and run `snapshot diff monday-baseline today` to see the week`s movement.

recipes.title: Find keyword opportunities your competitor ranks for
recipes.command: semrush-pp-cli keyword gap myco.com competitor.com --database us --kd-max 40 --json --select phrase,nq,kd
recipes.explanation: Set-difference query in one shot — narrowed to keywords with realistic difficulty that the competitor ranks for but you don't.

recipes.title: Build a link-prospect list from a competitor
recipes.command: semrush-pp-cli backlink gap myco.com competitor.com --min-ascore 70 --json --select domain,domain_ascore,backlinks_num
recipes.explanation: Authority-70+ referring domains linking to the competitor but not to you, ready to pipe into an outreach workflow.

recipes.title: Triage the latest Site Audit
recipes.explanation: Once a Site Audit snapshot exists, get the 20 highest-impact pages weighted by errors x3 + warnings x1 + notices x0.1.

recipes.title: Where did this month's credits go
recipes.explanation: Local credit_log rolled up by command; surfaces the top spenders so you can throttle or cache them.

trigger_phrases: show me what changed for <domain> this week
find the keyword gap between <my domain> and <competitor>
show me new referring domains for <domain>
what did Semrush cost me this month
